spec_match.rationale: Scans for h1 nested in article/aside/nav/section and surfaces author CSS rules that depended on the UA cascade.

interactive.rationale: 2 interactive markers — minimal. Paste-and-analyse is interactive but light.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises paste-and-flag; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't seed with real-world examples (Bootstrap, Tailwind defaults, popular templates). A pre-loaded sample would make the tool useful out of the gate.

openQuestions.title: Seed with popular template snippets
openQuestions.detail: Pre-fill the textarea with Bootstrap card markup, Tailwind hero layouts, and a WordPress block library sample. Visitors immediately see real-world breakage instead of needing to find their own.

openQuestions.title: Output remediation snippets
openQuestions.detail: When the scanner flags an h1, output a recommended CSS replacement that restores the visual hierarchy without the UA cascade. Currently it flags but doesn't fix.

summary: Useful tooling shape but ships empty — needs seeded examples and recommended fixes to be picked up by an audit run.
